title: Buildprint Mapper OS
creator: Agent Buildprint
stack: agb runner: persona, state derive, drift check, slice status
summary: Agent-session workflow for mapping an existing repo into an executable, source-independent v2 Slice/Gate Buildprint packet that resists agent self-certification through persona injection, derived state, and mechanical drift checks.
plainDescription: Use Mapper OS when you have a working repo and want an agent to turn it into a reusable, anti-fabrication Buildprint: map the source, classify the artifact type, choose a scope, then extract a v2 packet of vertical slices and horizontal gates that a fresh coding agent (using the agb runner) can implement with product judgment, hostile-reviewer acceptance, derived state, and honest blockers — without reading the original code.
promise: A repo-to-Buildprint workflow that preserves scope fidelity, classifies artifact type, emits a v2 Slice/Gate packet, and ships the agb runner contract that mechanically blocks self-certification, fake providers, and stale acceptance results.
whatYouGet: A selected-buildprint/ v2 executable packet: BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, 02-architecture.md, 03-ux-contract.md, blueprint.yaml, slices/, gates/, 04-handover.md

Typed setup that names artifact mode, real consumer, central artifact/interface/boundary, first usable loop, persistence/traces/readback, boundaries, fake-feel risks, local commands, and forbidden shortcuts
03-ux-contract.md as the single Path Map truth, with novice and operator (sample_can_satisfy: false) acceptance rows
Vertical slices with paths, core_proof_required, and persona references into templates/teams/ capsules
Horizontal gates with active_when_posture rules and human-signoff requirements
agb runner commands: persona (system-prompt builder), state derive (no agent self-write), drift check (mechanical tripwires), slice status (human-readable report)
Concise generated handover with derived overall status and continue-from-here menu
whatYouNeed: GitHub/private repo access if the source is private
A human scope choice when multiple candidates exist
Permission to use any private source code
includes: BUILDPRINT.md execution start and read order

02-project-setup.md artifact mode, consumer, first loop, central artifact/interface/boundary, persistence/traces/readback, boundaries, fake-feel risks, commands, quality rules, and forbidden shortcuts
03-ux-contract.md global Path Map and acceptance rows (novice + operator)
blueprint.yaml v2 schema with posture-aware agent_contract
slices/ vertical work units with paths, core_proof_required, persona ref
gates/ horizontal posture-conditional checks with human signoff
templates/teams/ capsule files used as persona system prompts
risks: Packet overfits source internals instead of observable behavior
Generated phases become generic dashboard work
Agent writes proof-looking prose instead of building a usable loop
Provider/deployment/security boundaries are hidden instead of named
Non-product artifacts are forced into product-app UI phases
checks: Selected output uses v2 packet with BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, 02-architecture.md, 03-ux-contract.md, blueprint.yaml, slices/, gates/, 04-handover.md
blueprint.yaml declares schema_version mapper-os/executable-blueprint/v2, deployment_posture, slices_dir, gates_dir, capsules_dir, and the partial-not-complete agent_contract rule
03-ux-contract.md provides a Path Map and at least one operator (sample_can_satisfy: false) acceptance row
Every slice.yaml#paths id traces to 03-ux-contract.md Path Map
Packet avoids the v1 phase chain (03-phases/, 04-review.md, 05-handover.md, generated/agent-prompt.md)
agb drift check passes (no state self-write, no fake provider, no stale contract version, no plaintext secrets)
Gates with requires_human_signoff: true block promotion until a non-agent signoff_by is recorded

trustBadges.label: Anti-self-certification
trustBadges.detail: state.json is derived by agb state derive from acceptance results; agents that try to write it directly are blocked by drift check.

trustBadges.label: Hostile reviewer
trustBadges.detail: Acceptance runs in a separate session loaded with the acceptance-hostile-reviewer capsule; sample paths and blockers cannot satisfy core_proof_required.

trustBadges.label: Persona injection
trustBadges.detail: Each slice session is opened with a capsule from templates/teams/ via agb persona, anchoring the agent's identity to a stable file reference.

trustBadges.label: Path Map traceability
trustBadges.detail: 03-ux-contract.md is the single source for path ids; agb drift check rejects any slice that invents paths.

trustBadges.label: Posture-aware gates
trustBadges.detail: Horizontal gates activate per deployment posture; gates with human signoff need a non-agent signoff_by before passing.

howToUse.title: 1. Point it at a repo
howToUse.detail: Open the source repo in your coding agent, or give the agent a repo URL/local path. If the target is unclear, Mapper OS must ask exactly: Which repo URL or local path should I map?

howToUse.title: 2. Bootstrap the Mapper OS package
howToUse.detail: Run agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json and follow .buildprint/next-agent.md. Do not use an agb map command.

howToUse.title: 3. Discover, then choose scope
howToUse.detail: The agent inspects the repo and proposes candidates. For broad repos, it stops and asks which candidate to extract. Smaller scope is fine; vague whole-repo sludge is not.

howToUse.title: 4. Extract the v2 packet
howToUse.detail: The selected packet contains BUILDPRINT.md, 02-architecture.md, 03-ux-contract.md, blueprint.yaml, slices/, gates/, and 04-handover.md. Path ids in slices must trace to the ux-contract Path Map.

howToUse.title: 5. Drive implementation with agb
howToUse.detail: Per slice: agb persona --role build, builder implements, agb persona --role acceptance, acceptance writes acceptance-result.json, agb state derive, agb drift check, agb slice status.

resultChecklist: Selected scope and source-independent artifact promise
Artifact mode, real consumer, central artifact/interface/boundary, and first usable loop
03-ux-contract.md Path Map with at least one operator (sample_can_satisfy: false) acceptance row per user-facing path
slices/<id>/slice.yaml with paths, core_proof_required, persona reference
gates/gate-index.yaml with active_when_posture rules and human signoff requirements
Mode-appropriate handling for UI, API/library, integration, automation, data-pipeline, and infrastructure surfaces
Honest blockers for credentials, deployment, destructive actions, paid services, and security review
copyPrompt: Use the Buildprint Mapper OS Buildprint to map a target repo in the current agent session. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. Before discovery, resolve the target: if no repository URL, local path, or already-opened working repo is given, ask exactly one blocking question — 'Which repo URL or local path should I map?' — and do not start until it is explicit.
